110471.explanation: VVD Best aims for the best candidates, regardless of gender.

110472.explanation: VVD Best, as a representative body, is always accessible to residents, entrepreneurs, and organizations. We always actively engage in discussions on important topics.

110475.explanation: Secondary activities are allowed but must be coordinated with the surroundings and comply with good spatial planning.

110476.explanation: VVD Best believes the municipality should encourage such projects but that financial support is not always necessary.

110478.explanation: VVD Best believes this should happen if it is a legal obligation, but it should be small-scale and fitting the environment.

110481.explanation: A good regulation and information campaign must be established to prevent nuisance about when burning is allowed or not.

110486.explanation: This is a legal obligation of the entrepreneurs themselves. The municipality can better supervise this.

110487.explanation: VVD Best advocates good spatial planning and from there reviews where there is room to build. Other infrastructure, such as roads and sewers, must also be in order.

110489.explanation: The local broadcaster is important for providing information at the local level, but the number of activities they carry out must increase accordingly.

110494.explanation: Safety is extremely important to residents and the enforcement officers (boa's) can contribute to a feeling of safety.

110495.explanation: It constitutes an intrusion into our residents' privacy. It is only acceptable if demonstrably necessary and temporary.

110498.explanation: VVD Best wants to make maximum use of arrangements to give priority to local residents and people connected to Best, such as students living away.

110500.explanation: In the current tight housing market, this can create additional living space.

110501.explanation: There is a great need for housing and suitable and large locations are located outside the built-up area.

110502.explanation: Work must be appropriate and meaningful. It contributes to participation in society, prevents isolation, and, where possible, promotes reintegration. This requires care and customization.

110504.explanation: This must be part of better tailored support, in connection with all applicable regulations.

110506.explanation: VVD Best stimulates home charging by allowing the installation of cable ducts. There are currently sufficient charging options in Best.

110507.explanation: In the neighborhoods, the speed limit is already 30 km/h, and for smooth traffic flow in Best, 50 km/h roads are needed. Adjusting road design to 30 km/h involves high costs.

110508.explanation: Free choice of healthcare provider already exists in the Netherlands. For the municipality, quality of care is paramount.

110513.explanation: In Best, there is hardly adjacent space to expand. The current layout of the business parks may possibly be adjusted.

110514.explanation: If there is demand from entrepreneurs.

110515.explanation: Status holders receive no specific priority but participate under the existing policy for allocation of social rental housing.

110516.explanation: VVD Best wants to maintain the current scheme, where private individuals can dispose of waste for free several times a year.

110517.explanation: Yes, in consultation with the surroundings and fitting within spatial planning, with attention to integration into the landscape and the promotion of nature (for example biodiversity).

110518.explanation: The VVD Best supports integration in the building for the new construction of the adjacent school.

110520.explanation: In Best, we have sufficient facilities for swimming lessons that can be used during school hours. It is up to the schools to decide whether they want to schedule this during school time.

110521.explanation: VVD Best believes this is not a task for the municipality. It is the schools' choice to pay attention to this.

110525.explanation: A maximum number must be established, and the rooms must meet certain (safety) requirements.

110526.explanation: Accessibility and the availability of the center are very important to attract people to our center.

110529.explanation: Not everyone wants to live small and cheap. Best should primarily build where there is demand, while considering future housing turnover and the quality of the living environment.

110472.explanation: The referendum is not always the best method; there are other (more effective) ways of citizen participation where all input is heard.

110475.explanation: We must handle the rural area carefully. This means we cannot allow shops and campsites everywhere, because that results in cluttering.

110476.explanation: We find it important to contribute to the energy transition. Good for the environment and the wallet. This can be done with loans at favorable interest rates.

110478.explanation: As far as D66 Best is concerned, we comply with the applicable distribution law. This means we seek sufficient shelter places. This does not have to be large-scale (one asylum seekers center), but preferably smaller scale. This can better guarantee connection and support in the surrounding area.

110481.explanation: We understand that wood-burning can cause nuisance. We support clear instructions for the use of stoves and fireplaces. A general ban is difficult to enforce, as has been shown in other municipalities.

110486.explanation: Entrepreneurs are primarily responsible for complying with the UN Convention on Human Rights. As a municipality, we have a stimulating role. Financing this is basically not a municipal task, but in case of bottlenecks, the municipality can provide financial support.

110487.explanation: By considering developments in the rural area together, we can use the scarce space in our municipality carefully and sustainably.

110489.explanation: D66 Best sees opportunities for collaboration with other local broadcasters and does not see extra funding as the first solution to strengthen the broadcaster. However, D66 Best endorses the importance of a local broadcaster.

110494.explanation: At some locations, there is significant nuisance, making the current deployment of public order officers not always sufficient. D66 Best finds it important that rules are properly enforced.

110495.explanation: In Best, camera surveillance is not yet necessary at this time.

110498.explanation: With social rental homes, all urgent applicants are given priority, such as a single mother with a child, people with medical emergencies, or those who have been granted a status. Some new housing projects explicitly state that residents of the local municipality are given priority in allocation.

110500.explanation: Permanent residence in holiday homes undermines the recreational function, does not structurally solve the housing shortage, and sometimes leads to reduced livability and enforcement problems.

110501.explanation: Expansion beyond village boundaries is sometimes necessary to address the housing shortage, provided this is done carefully and sustainably, with attention to social facilities, nature, and landscape.

110502.explanation: D66 Best opposes mandatory counter-performance but supports voluntary participation and guidance to work, appropriate to someone's situation.

110504.explanation: D66 Best agrees with this, because people just above the current income threshold also struggle to make ends meet and otherwise fall between the cracks. Efforts must be made for the safety net for the so-called working poor.

110506.explanation: Some neighborhoods lack charging stations for electric cars. The municipality should investigate where expansion is needed.

110507.explanation: A general 30 km/h limit is not suitable everywhere, and D66 Best prefers street-by-street customization for a good balance between safety, traffic flow, and livability, as is already the practice.

110508.explanation: Freedom of choice in healthcare is important. This must, of course, remain within the limits of quality, availability, and equality.

110513.explanation: New business parks put additional pressure on scarce space and landscape. Therefore, D66 Best focuses on better use and sustainability of existing sites so that space remains available for housing and nature.

110514.explanation: All Sundays are already shopping Sundays, but the hours may be extended if the entrepreneur wishes.

110515.explanation: Not only status holders are given urgent priority, but especially also single mothers with children, residents with medical urgency, and others in dire situations.

110516.explanation: There is already a generous maximum, and that must be maintained. For some waste streams, a contribution is requested for processing costs.

110517.explanation: A decision framework has been made for solar panels on land. The preference is for solar panels on roofs.

110518.explanation: We have approved a beautiful new multifunctional facility for both the school and the Kadans community center.

110520.explanation: School swimming increases children's safety and helps prevent swimming deficiencies.

110521.explanation: The municipality does not decide the content of the curriculum. We leave that to the education sector itself.

110525.explanation: Co-housing and dividing houses help reduce the housing shortage. If residents want this, it should be possible.

110526.explanation: The bicycle must be made more attractive by investing in better cycling facilities. At the same time, the center is often very busy with cars. If new developments cause parking spaces to disappear, these must be compensated within current parking standards.

110529.explanation: D66 Best sees new senior homes as an important driver for housing turnover. These are not only small homes but rather more spacious and barrier-free homes. Single starters might need smaller apartments, which should also be built.

110471.explanation: JO wants a council that reflects society. Equal representation ensures diversity, fairer decisions, and more trust. This aligns with national trends and youth aspirations. Clear selection criteria are crucial.

110472.explanation: JO wants to give residents a say via referenda. This increases involvement and ensures decisions are widely supported.

110475.explanation: JO supports farmers' side activities such as shops or campsites. This strengthens the countryside and provides additional income, provided nature and tranquility are preserved.

110476.explanation: JO wants support for joint energy generation, for example through subsidies. First utilize roofs, then only the rural area.

110478.explanation: JO does not want large-scale asylum seeker centers. Reception should be small-scale and local, where newcomers, starters, and elderly live and integrate together. First look at regional distribution to offer fair housing opportunities for the people from Best.

110481.explanation: JO advocates clear information about responsible heating and stimulating alternatives, so that living comfort is maintained without harming others' health. Clear communication about rules and enforcement is essential.

110486.explanation: JO considers inclusion and accessibility essential. In Best, there are still buildings that are not well accessible for people with disabilities. By financially supporting entrepreneurs, it becomes feasible for them to make adjustments. This increases independence.

110487.explanation: JO sees that farmers in Best sometimes want to continue living and working on their own land, even if they stop the business. Limited building opportunities cause vacancy and decline. JO advocates for customization: farmers should be able to build homes under conditions.

110489.explanation: Nationally, local broadcasters are transitioning to regional broadcasters. In Best, this means Studio 040 becomes our regional broadcaster. JO wants the municipality to support the local broadcaster with extra funding to ensure local information provision.

110494.explanation: Safety is a basic requirement for a pleasant living environment. JO hears from society that there is a need for more visible enforcement, for example regarding nuisance or traffic safety. More community enforcement officers can contribute to a safer Best, provided deployment is well coordinated.

110495.explanation: Camera surveillance can help prevent and solve crime, but also affects privacy. JO believes camera surveillance should only be used where really necessary, for example in places with much nuisance or crime. Transparency is essential.

110498.explanation: JO understands that residents want to continue living in their own environment. Priority for local housing seekers can contribute to social cohesion and the preservation of networks. At the same time, policy must be fair and transparent.

110500.explanation: JO sees that the housing shortage in Best calls for creative solutions. Allowing permanent residence in holiday homes can provide temporary relief. However, the municipality must set clear rules to prevent abuse and nuisance.

110501.explanation: JO wants to preserve the green character of Best and chooses infill over expansion. By building within village boundaries, the surrounding area remains protected and sealing of land is prevented. This aligns with the wish of many residents to preserve nature and landscape.

110502.explanation: JO finds it important that everyone can participate in society. Socially meaningful work provides structure, social contacts, and contributes to self-confidence. In Best, there are projects where welfare recipients do volunteer work or help in the neighborhood.

110504.explanation: JO wants to help more people who have financial difficulties. By raising the income threshold, workers with low incomes also qualify for support. In Best, there are various arrangements, but not everyone knows how to find them.

110506.explanation: JO stimulates sustainable mobility and sees that the number of electric cars in Best is growing. More charging stations make electric driving more attractive and contribute to climate goals. Clear communication about locations and use of charging points is important.

110507.explanation: JO values traffic safety, especially for vulnerable groups such as children and the elderly. Lowering the maximum speed to 30 km/h reduces accidents and makes the residential environment more pleasant. In Best, there are already 30 km/h zones around schools.

110508.explanation: JO stands for freedom of choice and respect for diversity. People with different backgrounds and beliefs live in Best. It is important that everyone can receive appropriate care. Clear communication about choices and rights is essential.

110513.explanation: JO chooses a strong local economy but wants to allow expansion of business parks only if it fits sustainability and livability. Cooperation between entrepreneurs and municipality is important to prevent nuisance.

110514.explanation: JO sees that shopping Sundays contribute to vibrancy and economic growth. In Best, there is demand for more flexibility for retailers and consumers. Clear communication about rules and options is important to prevent misunderstandings.

110515.explanation: JO believes vulnerable groups, including refugees, should be helped with integration. Priority in social housing can contribute to a good start but must be fairly balanced against the interests of other housing seekers.

110516.explanation: JO wants to prevent litter and illegal dumping. Free waste disposal at the recycling center lowers the threshold and contributes to a clean municipality. Clear communication about rules and opening hours is important.

110517.explanation: JO first chooses solar panels on roofs but sees that farmland sometimes offers opportunities for sustainable energy. Double use, such as panels above parking spaces, can be a solution. Clear communication about locations and conditions is necessary.

110518.explanation: JO finds meeting places important for social cohesion. The new Multifunctional Accommodation (MFA), into which Buurthuis Kadans will merge, can contribute to more activities and involvement in the neighborhood. Clear communication about plans and financing is essential.

110520.explanation: JO finds swimming skills important for safety and health. Municipal support makes school swimming accessible to all children. Clear communication about participation and costs is necessary.

110521.explanation: JO values local culture and traditions. Attention to the Brabant language and culture at school strengthens identity and the sense of belonging. Clear communication with schools is important.

110525.explanation: JO believes room rentals offer opportunities for young people and students but wants to prevent nuisance and overcrowding in residential neighborhoods. Clear communication about rules and supervision is essential.

110526.explanation: JO stimulates sustainable mobility and wants to make the center more attractive for pedestrians and cyclists. Fewer parking spaces can contribute to more greenery and space for social interaction. Clear communication about alternatives is important.

110529.explanation: JO sees the demand for small and affordable housing is great, especially among young people and the elderly. By focusing on this, flow is promoted, and more people get a chance at housing. Clear communication about new construction plans is important.

110471.explanation: Diversity leads to better decision-making and ensures equal representation. We strive for equality, but quality must be decisive.

110472.explanation: An advisory referendum can be a nice and fair instrument to gauge opinion. This way we involve more people in local democracy.

110475.explanation: This requires customization, so we say 'yes, unless.' It is not feasible everywhere in the spatial environment; not every location is suitable for a camping or shop. We want to make agriculture more sustainable, and this can contribute to that.

110476.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly generate their own green and affordable electricity. They use the electricity in the same place, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

110478.explanation: Best currently does not receive any asylum seekers. The distribution law is still in force, so Best is obliged to host asylum seekers. We believe we take our moral and legal responsibility. As a municipality, we can and must contribute to hosting asylum seekers.

110481.explanation: The biggest sources of fine particles, besides traffic, are fireplaces and wood stoves. Therefore, we do not want wood burning in new constructions. This contributes to a healthier living environment. We are not (yet) in favor of a total ban.

110486.explanation: Accessibility is very important for people with disabilities. Many places are not accessible, so we support entrepreneurs working on this. Inclusive building should be the norm, which is why we want this included in the building regulations. For existing buildings, we work together.

110487.explanation: We must do everything to reduce the housing shortage. Building on own land can help, provided it fits well into the environment. The municipality must carefully assess this; it is therefore customization.

110489.explanation: Local media are important to know what is happening in Best. It is about stories from the street to the city council. Investing contributes to a strong and accessible democracy, so we support this.

110494.explanation: Best is growing; additional enforcement officers belong to this. For that reason, we support expansion.

110495.explanation: Extra camera surveillance must be proportional as it affects privacy. We currently see no reason to expand this.

110498.explanation: Residents from the municipality of Best can also respond to social housing in other municipalities; social rent is thus for everyone who needs it. We do support priority for informal caregivers and other urgent seekers who are tied to Best.

110500.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution for the housing shortage, but as long as the need is high, people can use them. Therefore, we support this.

110501.explanation: We primarily focus on infill, thus within the existing core. Our municipality and region are growing so fast that we must look for new large-scale housing locations. We actively search for promising locations.

110502.explanation: Participation is important, but should not become a mandatory counterperformance. Residents receiving social assistance may have various reasons (such as their health) that make a counterperformance currently unfeasible and actually cause extra stress. This requires a tailored approach.

110504.explanation: We want to raise the threshold of all poverty schemes to 130% of the social assistance level. This would allow more residents to benefit from income support. Currently, too many low-income residents fall outside the system; raising the income limit will enable us to help more residents.

110506.explanation: We are putting the transition to electric driving into top gear. Our party aims to double the number of charging stations, including parking spaces.

110507.explanation: 30 km/h is safer and improves livability. However, not all roads (such as the ring road) are suitable for this speed. We want 30 km/h in more places, but not everywhere within the built-up area.

110508.explanation: There is a shortage of care. Preferably, residents should be able to choose, but care must first and foremost match the care demand. That should be the starting point for us; if the choice exists, then we support it. We do not consider it a good idea to make it mandatory.

110513.explanation: Business parks may expand, provided this fits spatially and does not take away space for housing. Expansion is important to ensure a future-proof economy. We focus on high-quality companies that add value to the municipality and region.

110514.explanation: Residents and entrepreneurs should have the freedom to choose when they want to shop.

110515.explanation: The asylum process is stuck and flow must continue. Municipalities are obliged to house status holders; this is only possible while retaining the priority arrangement. At the same time, we must build significantly more affordable homes. Starters, families, and status holders all feel the housing crisis.

110516.explanation: It is already possible to make some disposals free of charge. Making it completely free would undermine waste separation and the waste disposal fees. We support a system where the polluter pays.

110517.explanation: Solar energy is essential to become climate-neutral. We first focus on panels on roofs, along highways, and other suitable locations. Using agricultural land is a last option according to the solar ladder and should be combined with biodiversity so that flowers and greenery can grow.

110518.explanation: Wilhelminadorp deserves a new community center. With the new MFA, a beautiful facility will come to the neighborhood where education, childcare, the community center, and day activities come together. We support this!

110520.explanation: The Netherlands is a water country and every child should learn to swim. Including children whose parents cannot afford swimming lessons. Unfortunately, this is still not fairly arranged. The best solution: school swimming for every child, with support from the municipality.

110521.explanation: Municipalities do not decide on the content of education. If education does provide this, the municipality has no role in it.

110525.explanation: We support splitting homes, but separate room rentals are often misused to house labor migrants under poor conditions. Therefore, we want to prevent such rooming.

110526.explanation: The center can become more attractive, but choices must be made. We want less space for cars and more for residents, allowing for nice terraces. Underground parking can also contribute.

110529.explanation: The housing shortage is enormous, with the greatest need for affordable and smaller homes. There are also increasingly more singles, but our housing supply is not adjusted to this.

110471.explanation: We aim for quality and availability. Best Open has a female candidate for alderman.

110472.explanation: Participation is very important. A referendum goes too far for us.

110475.explanation: Mandates are not an option. But there may be possibilities. This is already possible in certain situations.

110476.explanation: Support must be available. However, support can also be provided without funding.

110478.explanation: Within the possibilities, we comply with the distribution law. Humane asylum reception is desirable to us.

110481.explanation: Yes to promoting reduction in use. This is in the context of public health.

110486.explanation: The building must be accessible to the target group. Effort is required from both the operator and the owner of the building.

110487.explanation: Laws and regulations do not permit this.

110489.explanation: Nothing extra. But maintain current support.

110494.explanation: For safety, yes. BOAs (officials with enforcement authority) should be more visible in unsafe situations.

110495.explanation: Only if it is truly necessary.

110500.explanation: We are not in favor of increasing permanent residence in rural areas.

110501.explanation: First build within existing limits, then expand. Otherwise, we build too few homes. Expansion should respect our limited countryside, and new large locations should only be chosen/indicated after the merger with Oirschot.

110502.explanation: Participating in society is of great value. Those people benefit from that. Together with them, we seek a (meaningful) approach.

110504.explanation: We are currently at 120% of the social assistance standard with all arrangements, like many other municipalities.

110506.explanation: Acting wisely. Growing along with the need.

110507.explanation: Not everywhere. In residential areas is fine.

110508.explanation: We support freedom of religion and respect the constitution in this regard.

110513.explanation: No room for expansion. But improving current business parks.

110514.explanation: If there is demand from shopkeepers.

110516.explanation: The current rules are fine. Many things are now free.

110517.explanation: With good participation from the local community. But no 'overkill'.

110518.explanation: Community center activities will move to the new Multifunctional Accommodation (MFA) next to the current Kadans.

110520.explanation: Introduction. Support for those who cannot afford it.

110521.explanation: For us, learning to read and write Dutch is paramount. However, attention to one's own living area is not wrong.

110525.explanation: Within the existing frameworks. Such as not causing nuisance to street/neighborhood residents, parking possibilities, numbers, etc.

110526.explanation: We advocate for maintaining the current number of parking spaces.

110471.explanation: We strive for a diverse executive board, with quality remaining the top priority.

110472.explanation: Of course, we find it important that residents can provide input through participation.

110475.explanation: This is always customized in consultation with the municipality.

110476.explanation: There are national subsidies and this depends on the situation.

110478.explanation: We take our responsibility in the reception of asylum seekers. This can be either on small locations or a large location. Research must determine what it will be.

110481.explanation: At orange and red code levels, we want wood stoves not to be used.

110486.explanation: Buildings must be easily accessible for people with disabilities. This is the entrepreneur's responsibility.

110487.explanation: When this fits within the environmental plan, we do see possibilities, but not as a general right.

110494.explanation: At the moment, we see no reason for more enforcement officers, but we do not exclude it for the future.

110495.explanation: In specific areas, such as industrial estates, additional camera surveillance can contribute to safety.

110498.explanation: We find it important that home seekers from Best get the opportunity to stay in Best.

110500.explanation: These are a different kind of homes, with a different designation. They are not intended for permanent residence.

110502.explanation: This is not always possible, but it must be encouraged.

110504.explanation: As a municipality, we have already set this limit at 120% of the minimum wage.

110506.explanation: We promote the installation of cable ducts to prevent parking spaces for electric cars from remaining empty.

110507.explanation: It is important to create good traffic flow within the municipality. For this, access roads are needed.

110508.explanation: We believe that everyone should be able to receive appropriate care, corresponding to the problem. Quality is paramount.

110513.explanation: Best has few expansion locations, so we find this inappropriate.

110514.explanation: At this moment, there is no need to make adjustments here.

110515.explanation: We must adhere to legal guidelines, but there are more target groups eligible for social rental housing and they also have the right to it.

110516.explanation: This is already free four times a year per household.

110517.explanation: Agricultural land is not suitable everywhere for placing solar panels, and the development of energy savings and generation is advancing so quickly that solar panels are no longer the only solution.

110518.explanation: Kadans will become part of the new multifunctional accommodation (MFA).

110520.explanation: The Leergeld Foundation can provide financial support to families who cannot afford swimming lessons.

110521.explanation: This is not a task of the municipality.

110526.explanation: We support encouraging visits to the center by bike or on foot, but there will always be residents who depend on the car to do their shopping or use the facilities.

110529.explanation: Building must be based on needs.

110471.explanation: When forming the executive board, we choose the best aldermen for Best. We look at qualities and involvement, not gender. Good representation is important, but the interest of Best comes first.

110472.explanation: Residents must be able to give their opinion on important issues. Referenda can help with this, provided it is clear in advance what the goal is and what happens with the outcome. It must not be sham participation, but a means to seriously involve residents in decision-making.

110475.explanation: The municipality should not always automatically allow this. However, we find it important to engage in dialogue with farmers about their plans. We assess per situation what suits the environment, livability, and the interests of local residents.

110476.explanation: We welcome generating sustainable energy together. For residents who can afford it themselves, subsidies are not necessary. For people or initiatives where money is a barrier, targeted financial support can help them participate. This keeps sustainability fair and affordable.

110478.explanation: The municipal council already made a decision about this in September 2024. The Christian Union voted in favor, based on Christian norms and values such as charity and justice. We find it important to keep agreements made and to organize reception in a humane and careful manner.

110481.explanation: The use of fireplaces and wood stoves can cause nuisance under certain weather conditions. Living together means considering each other. Therefore, no absolute ban or total freedom applies, but sensible use with attention to health and living environment.

110486.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate, including people with disabilities. The municipality is not responsible for financially supporting every entrepreneur in making adjustments. However, it can actively promote accessibility and we expect entrepreneurs to take their responsibility.

110487.explanation: The rural area is scarce and requires careful use of space. Housing construction on agricultural land cannot just happen. We are happy to talk to farmers about their situation, but building on own land is not automatic and must fit within spatial plans and the character of the rural area.

110489.explanation: We support a strong, professional regional broadcaster instead of extra funding for a small local broadcaster. This way, we combine forces, improve quality, and keep costs manageable. Local information remains important but should be provided in a future-proof form.

110494.explanation: There is currently no situation in Best that is so unsafe that extra enforcement officers are needed. The ChristenUnie stands for a livable municipality where living is pleasant. If the situation changes, we will reassess what is needed to ensure safety.

110495.explanation: Camera surveillance can be a useful tool in specific unsafe spots. But we do not favor more camera surveillance as a general measure. We do not want a society where everyone is constantly monitored; safety also requires trust and human presence.

110498.explanation: Housing allocation requires regional cooperation. Some housing seekers urgently need a home; in those cases, location is less relevant. If each municipality only prioritizes its own residents, this can also be disadvantageous for the residents of Best. Therefore, we choose solidarity and tailored solutions.

110500.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for temporary stays. As a temporary solution, such as in cases of urgency, use as a residential address can be appropriate. Permanent habitation is not a good solution; holiday homes should remain holiday homes.

110501.explanation: We need many homes. We support housing construction within the village boundaries, but there we simply run into (literal) limits. We simply need more homes.

110502.explanation: Participation is important and contributes to self-esteem and well-being. People receiving social assistance benefits can be expected to do socially meaningful work, always appropriate to their abilities. If they cannot, then they cannot. No one becomes happier by standing on the sidelines.

110504.explanation: Help with poverty is more than just money. Much poverty is hidden and requires timely identification and personal support. Raising income thresholds is not always the solution; it is more important to identify people in time and together see what is truly needed.

110506.explanation: More charging points are not useful everywhere, especially not in areas for short parking like blue zones. The municipality should be cautious here and focus mainly where it is really needed, considering efficient use of space and sustainable alternatives like cycling.

110507.explanation: Safety always comes first, but not every road serves the same function. In residential streets and places with many pedestrians and cyclists, 30 km/h is logical. On through roads, such as the Ring Road, 50 km/h is necessary for accessibility and smooth traffic flow.

110508.explanation: Freedom of choice in healthcare is not always possible; we are grateful for every healthcare professional. Everyone deserves respect regardless of faith or conviction. We also believe in caring communities where people support each other and there is attention for individual needs.

110513.explanation: We do not want to automatically expand industrial areas. First, existing sites and vacant buildings must be better utilized. Only when this space is demonstrably insufficient can expansion be considered. This way, we handle space and landscape carefully.

110514.explanation: Sunday is a day of rest and reflection. We grant residents and entrepreneurs a shared day off when you do not have to work. A 24/7 society does not automatically bring more well-being. Rest, meeting others, and balance are at least as important for a healthy society.

110515.explanation: As a municipality, we have agreements about housing status holders, and we must honor those. We find that important. At the same time, priority is not only for status holders but also for other people with urgent housing needs. It is about fair and careful distribution.

110516.explanation: The current system works well: most waste can already be disposed of free of charge. That encourages proper waste separation and prevents littering and illegal dumping. The municipality must maintain this accessibility.

110517.explanation: We support the Solar Ladder: first install solar panels on roofs of homes, businesses, and other buildings. Solar panels are important, but we prefer not to use valuable farmland for energy production but rather for food production and landscape preservation.

110518.explanation: The primary school Bricks will get new construction, in which Kadans will be housed. It will be a multifunctional facility.

110520.explanation: We want every child to learn to swim but do not believe that mandatory school swimming is necessary for that; targeted support (for example, if people cannot afford it) works better.

110521.explanation: In this increasingly international region, it is much more important to pay attention to the diversity of cultures.

110525.explanation: Room rental can contribute to affordable housing for starters and singles. The municipality should allow this, but with clear rules to prevent nuisance and exploitation and to keep neighborhoods livable.

110526.explanation: The number of parking spaces is not a goal in itself. ChristenUnie wants to organize parking smarter: no more than necessary, but also no less than necessary. Accessibility of the center for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs remains important.

110529.explanation: Many young people and starters cannot move out of their parents’ home now. Therefore, the focus in new construction should be on smaller and affordable homes.

110471.explanation: Best-Anders looks at what you can do, not who you are. We choose the best candidate, man or woman!

110472.explanation: We are open to a referendum, but only if it directly affects your own living environment. It is therefore not a vote for all residents at once; we look at who may vote per topic.

110475.explanation: Why not? We think it's fine if farmers start a shop or campsite. The condition is that the immediate neighbors have no objections. If you communicate well and make clear agreements, much is possible. Good consultation is the basis.

110476.explanation: But it must be fair. Support should depend on your income, not only for the wealthy. We especially want the municipality to require housing corporations to make homes energy-efficient. This will lower energy bills for people in social rental and more affordable owner-occupied homes.

110478.explanation: No, not now. Because of the merger in 2028, we will soon have a municipality that already has a reception center. We think it smarter to expand that location rather than build a new center in Best now. We are not against newcomers, but for now, we prioritize the well-being of our own community.

110481.explanation: We do not want a ban. The municipality should not interfere too much in your private life. However, we do want clean air. That is why we opt for technology: better chimneys and filters that clean the smoke. This way, the neighborhood does not suffer from odors or soot.

110486.explanation: For new construction, it is simple: you pay for the adaptations yourself and it is mandatory. For the sale of an existing property, it is also mandatory, but then the municipality contributes via subsidies. This way, Best becomes increasingly accessible without unnecessarily burdening entrepreneurs.

110487.explanation: Building a house or houses is always in consultation with neighbors and the municipality.

110489.explanation: No extra money. We only pay what is mandatory. It is not good for the municipality to pay the local broadcaster. That is why we appreciate that the national government will arrange this in the future. That is much better for journalistic independence.

110494.explanation: Best Anders does not want simply more enforcement officers but better choices. Current enforcers should focus less on parking and more on real safety and nuisance in the neighborhood. It is not about the number of officers but about the right priorities for a safe Best.

110495.explanation: Unfortunately, camera surveillance is necessary nowadays. Best Anders is therefore not against the use of cameras. Privacy is important, but the safety of our residents comes first. If cameras help to make Best safer, we support that, but only in specific situations.

110498.explanation: Absolutely! The waiting list is now 14 years. By the time you are next, you often earn too much. Best Anders wants to give priority to our own residents. We must first solve our own problems. That means: building for people from Best!

110500.explanation: Yes, at this moment, it is necessary. As long as the housing shortage is not resolved, the municipality must be more flexible. People need a roof over their heads. Therefore, Best Anders thinks it should be allowed to temporarily live in a holiday home until enough regular houses are available.

110501.explanation: In principle, we want to keep the rural area green. But we must be realistic: houses are needed. Best Anders believes that new construction outside the boundaries may be allowed under conditions if this is the only way to solve the housing shortage. Housing comes first.

110502.explanation: Yes, doing something in return for your benefit is normal in our view. But Best Anders believes the work must fit the person. We carefully consider age and what someone can do. It must be feasible and not put someone in trouble. Tailored solutions come first.

110504.explanation: Yes, because everything is becoming more expensive. A euro is worth less now than before. Best Anders thinks the income threshold should rise with the prices in shops. Otherwise, people who really need help fall through the cracks. The rules must fit the reality of today.

110506.explanation: Yes, that is necessary. More and more electric cars are on the road. Best Anders thinks the municipality should support this. More charging stations enable people to choose a clean car. This fits a green and modern municipality.

110507.explanation: Good idea, as long as you really talk about the center.

110508.explanation: For Best Anders only one thing matters: is the care good? That is what it’s all about. We do not believe the municipality should arrange that a doctor matches your faith. That is private. We ensure that the care is expert and available to everyone.

110513.explanation: Option 1: Focus on prosperity Why not? Economic growth cannot be stopped, and that is good for our prosperity. If there is still room, Best Anders says: go for it! More companies mean jobs and income. As long as it fits, we certainly allow business parks to grow.

110514.explanation: We live in a free country.

110515.explanation: Absolutely not! The housing shortage is severe. Best Anders finds it unfair that newcomers get priority while long-term residents of Best have to wait for years. We want to take care of the people from our own village first. That is not unwillingness, but choosing for your own citizens.

110516.explanation: Residents of Best already pay enough for waste; this service should, in our opinion, be free.

110517.explanation: We say no. Agricultural land is for growing food. First use the empty roofs of companies and houses. We also wonder: is this really the solution? Best Anders thinks sun and wind alone are not enough to solve all our energy problems.

110518.explanation: This is urgently needed, and we are already working on it here in Best.

110520.explanation: This should actually be organized by the schools. The municipality should contribute to this.

110521.explanation: It is important to know something about the environment where you live. So a few hours per year is fine by us.

110525.explanation: We do not want this in normal residential streets. That attracts slumlords and is bad for the neighborhood. But it is different with company housing. Best Anders believes we should ease the rules there. Converting those properties is a much better solution.

110526.explanation: I think there are already too few!

110529.explanation: Not especially, but as indicated before certainly many more.

statements.title: The municipality of Best must primarily build small and affordable homes.
The residents of Best must be able to give their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Best moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves must remain permitted in Best.

titleParts.text: In Best moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on public safety must be deployed in Best.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
More CCTV surveillance must be installed in Best.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Brabant dialect.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The business parks in Best may be allowed to expand.
There should be fewer parking spaces in the centre.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Best must renovate Buurthuis Kadans.
